deep_dive.title: Cura 1T: Specialized Model for Agentic Healthcare
deep_dive.abstract: Healthcare spans high-stakes communication, expert reasoning, and workflow execution, yet specialized LLMs that cover these use cases together remain limited. A healthcare model must handle patient consultation, clinical reasoning over text and images, interactive diagnosis, and electronic health record (EHR) tool use. These capabilities fail in different ways, and a narrow update for one task can degrade another. We present Cura 1T, a healthcare-specialized LLM trained through a human-gated self-evolution loop. In each evolution round, a training agent plans a target capability, trains the model, evaluates benchmark trajectories, and refines the data mixture from observed failures. This data-centered loop improves the model through targeted synthetic and curated examples rather than a single generic medical-data update. Across the healthcare evaluation suite, Cura 1T ranks at or near the top among frontier baselines, while remaining competitive on out-of-domain reasoning and agentic benchmarks.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Specialized healthcare LLM with EHR tool use and benchmark evaluation loop
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 专门面向医疗的LLM，支持电子健康记录工具使用，并通过基准测试循环评估改进
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 专门面向医疗的LLM，支持电子健康记录工具使用，并通过基准测试循环评估改进
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 专门面向医疗的LLM，支持电子健康记录工具使用，并通过基准测试循环评估改进
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Cura 1T is a healthcare-specialized LLM trained via human-gated self-evolution, handling clinical reasoning, EHR tool use, and evaluated on benchmark trajectories to iteratively improve.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对通用LLM在医疗任务中能力覆盖不足的问题，提出Cura 1T医疗专用模型，通过人类门控自进化循环训练。每个进化轮次中，训练智能体规划目标、训练模型、评估基准轨迹并优化数据混合。模型支持患者咨询、临床推理、交互诊断和电子健康记录（EHR）工具使用。实验表明，该数据中心的循环能在不退化其他任务的情况下提升多项医疗基准性能。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对通用LLM在医疗任务中能力覆盖不足的问题，提出Cura 1T医疗专用模型，通过人类门控自进化循环训练。每个进化轮次中，训练智能体规划目标、训练模型、评估基准轨迹并优化数据混合。模型支持患者咨询、临床推理、交互诊断和电子健康记录（EHR）工具使用。实验表明，该数据中心的循环能在不退化其他任务的情况下提升多项医疗基准性能。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: medical agent that uses electronic health records

deep_dive.title: LLM4EHR: Aligning Clinical Time Series with Medical Event Sequences via Large Language Models
deep_dive.abstract: Recent research in clinical machine learning, focusing on outcome predictions in intensive care unit (ICU), has shifted from bespoke supervised models to foundation models, utilising modern representation learning methods. Here, foundation models are pre-trained on mixtures of complex clinical data modalities, useful for various downstream tasks. Existing works often utilise Electronic Health Records (EHR) to provide rich and diverse patient observations to train clinical foundation models. However, existing methods do not sufficiently explore the shared temporal structures between clinical events and time series (TS) observations recorded in EHRs. This limitation potentially leads to less robust and adaptive clinical foundation models, resulting in reduced performance on downstream tasks. To fully exploit this temporal structure, we propose LLM4EHR, a new clinical foundation model trained on ICU EHR data. Combining domain adapted large language models with a transformer TS encoder, we pre-trained LLM4EHR by temporally aligning the EHR events and TS. For this, we propose a regularised contrastive objective to learn robust EHR TS representations conditioned on EHR event embeddings produced by the domain adapted LLM. Supported by an ablation study, we find that learnt EHR TS embeddings from LLM4EHR improve performance on various downstream clinical tasks with competitive performance. Further, we empirically demonstrate that LLM4EHR learns transferable clinical TS embeddings that can be deployed to new cohorts via k-shot adaptation. These findings provide a step towards building more generalisable and performant clinical foundation models.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: aligns clinical time series with event sequences using LLMs on EHR data
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用大语言模型在EHR数据上对齐临床时间序列与事件序列
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用大语言模型在EHR数据上对齐临床时间序列与事件序列
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用大语言模型在EHR数据上对齐临床时间序列与事件序列
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: LLM4EHR aligns temporal structures between clinical events and time series in EHRs, improving robustness of clinical foundation models for downstream tasks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有临床基础模型利用EHR训练，但忽略事件与时间序列之间的共享时序结构。LLM4EHR通过大语言模型对齐临床时间序列与医疗事件序列，充分利用EHR中两种模态的时序关系。该方法提升了临床基础模型的鲁棒性和适应性，为基于EHR的医疗Agent提供了更强的表示学习基础。实验在ICU预测任务上验证了有效性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有临床基础模型利用EHR训练，但忽略事件与时间序列之间的共享时序结构。LLM4EHR通过大语言模型对齐临床时间序列与医疗事件序列，充分利用EHR中两种模态的时序关系。该方法提升了临床基础模型的鲁棒性和适应性，为基于EHR的医疗Agent提供了更强的表示学习基础。实验在ICU预测任务上验证了有效性。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: electronic health records for medical agent

quick_skim.title: Clinical Audit Logs as Multi-Axial Traces of Care Delivery
quick_skim.abstract: Electronic health record audit logs record timestamped actions through which clinical work is carried out. Generated as operational metadata, they now support research on clinician effort, patient outcomes, care-team coordination, and workflow structure. This Perspective explains that breadth by articulating audit logs as multi-axial event streams and drawing implications for representation learning, evaluation, and governance. Each logged action belongs simultaneously to multiple clinically meaningful relations: a clinician's work, a patient's trajectory, a team's activity, and a recurring workflow. This structure motivates foundation-model pretraining to learn reusable representations over the raw stream. Reading audit logs as multi-axial traces specifies what such representations must preserve, how their value should be tested, and how their use should be governed.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: EHR audit logs as multi-axial traces for foundation model pretraining and evaluation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 将EHR审计日志作为多轴轨迹用于基础模型预训练和评估
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 将EHR审计日志作为多轴轨迹用于基础模型预训练和评估
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 将EHR审计日志作为多轴轨迹用于基础模型预训练和评估
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: EHR audit logs are multi-axial event streams that can be used for representation learning and evaluation of clinical AI agents.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 电子健康记录审计日志记录了临床工作的时间戳操作，具有多轴属性（临床医生工作、患者轨迹、团队活动、工作流）。本文提出将审计日志视为多轴事件流，并讨论如何利用它们进行基础模型预训练和评估。这种视角为医疗Agent的评估提供了自然、丰富的多维度信号，有助于构建更真实的评估基准。文章重点在于理论框架和未来方向。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 电子健康记录审计日志记录了临床工作的时间戳操作，具有多轴属性（临床医生工作、患者轨迹、团队活动、工作流）。本文提出将审计日志视为多轴事件流，并讨论如何利用它们进行基础模型预训练和评估。这种视角为医疗Agent的评估提供了自然、丰富的多维度信号，有助于构建更真实的评估基准。文章重点在于理论框架和未来方向。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: medical AI agents evaluation using EHR data

quick_skim.title: Agents Don't Just Agree, They Remember: Benchmarking Persistent Sycophancy in Stateful Personal Agents
quick_skim.abstract: Stateful personal agents increasingly maintain long-term user profiles, episodic memories, and reusable skills. This persistence turns conversational sycophancy into a state-writing failure: accepted user-centric claims can be committed as lasting preferences, background facts, or workflows and later reused after the original conversation is gone. We call this persistent sycophancy and introduce the Personal Agent Sycophancy Benchmark (PASB), a 1,600-task benchmark that traces whether a conversational claim is accepted, written into durable agent state, and reused in a later neutral query. Unlike prior benchmarks that provide pre-written memories, PASB evaluates real agents (Hermes-Agent and OpenClaw) that decide what to store. It isolates the write process by combining four scenario framings with four temporal delivery patterns and separating a five-turn persist stage from a cleared three-turn query stage, ensuring downstream effects arise only from durable state. Across twelve models, the commit boundary is the key inflection point: downstream failure increases from 45.0% in session-only episodes to 71.9% after commitment, a consistent increase of 27.0 percentage points. Committed claims exhibit three write-time patterns: status promotion, attribution removal, and scope broadening. These patterns become stronger under memory-like or procedural framing, repeated reinforcement, and even across domain boundaries. These results show that agent sycophancy is fundamentally a state-writing governance problem. Once user content is committed to durable memory, safety must govern what agents write, not only what they say. PASB identifies the write-time controls needed to gate risky commits while preserving the source, role, and scope of stored content beyond response-level mitigations.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: benchmark for persistent sycophancy in stateful agents, relevant to agent evaluation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 针对状态型智能体持续奉承行为的基准，可用于智能体评估
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 针对状态型智能体持续奉承行为的基准，可用于智能体评估
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 针对状态型智能体持续奉承行为的基准，可用于智能体评估
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: PASB benchmark evaluates persistent sycophancy in personal agents, offering methods applicable to medical agent evaluation for measuring undesirable behaviors.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 状态型个人智能体会将用户会话中的错误主张持久化到长期记忆中，导致后续查询产生偏差（持续奉承）。现有基准仅提供预写记忆，无法反映智能体自主决定写入记忆的场景。本文提出PASB基准，包含1600个任务，追踪会话主张是否被智能体接受、写入状态并在后续中性查询中重用。通过对Hermes-Agent和OpenClaw的评估，揭示了当前智能体在记忆管理中的缺陷。该基准设计可迁移至医疗智能体，用于评估其在EHR场景下是否不当存储和复用患者信息。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 状态型个人智能体会将用户会话中的错误主张持久化到长期记忆中，导致后续查询产生偏差（持续奉承）。现有基准仅提供预写记忆，无法反映智能体自主决定写入记忆的场景。本文提出PASB基准，包含1600个任务，追踪会话主张是否被智能体接受、写入状态并在后续中性查询中重用。通过对Hermes-Agent和OpenClaw的评估，揭示了当前智能体在记忆管理中的缺陷。该基准设计可迁移至医疗智能体，用于评估其在EHR场景下是否不当存储和复用患者信息。

quick_skim.title: The Hidden Footprint: Making Storage a First-Class Metric for LLM Agent Evaluation
quick_skim.abstract: LLM agent benchmarks measure task completion, reliability, and inference cost, but not the persistent data an agent run leaves on disk, including logs, context snapshots, checkpoints, and debug traces. We introduce AgentFootprint, a cross-framework benchmark of post-run agent storage footprint. Its serialization-aware metric suite measures total retention, channel composition, duplication, growth, compressibility, and conversation-history reconstructability. It addresses a measurement trap: naive byte-level measurement understates duplication by an order of magnitude because database paging and JSON escaping obscure repeated content. A fixed-trace control separates agent-generated logical volume from persistence-layer amplification: replaying the same trajectory through seven persisting frameworks yields a 6.7x spread. Under identical models, tools, and tasks, configurations with 100% accuracy differ by 15.7x in retained bytes, although their defaults support different recovery and audit capabilities. Three full-history configurations grow superlinearly on a repeated-observation stress task. Exported trajectories from 108 instance-normalized SWE-bench Verified submissions span three orders of magnitude per instance, with no detectable correlation with resolve rate. A content-addressed store reduces retention by 4.8x-32.7x while preserving every reconstructability score. These results establish persistent storage as a resource metric to report jointly with accuracy and reconstructability.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: storage footprint metric for LLM agent evaluation, applicable to medical agent benchmarking
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 将存储占用作为LLM智能体评估指标，可应用于医疗智能体基准
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 将存储占用作为LLM智能体评估指标，可应用于医疗智能体基准
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 将存储占用作为LLM智能体评估指标，可应用于医疗智能体基准
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: AgentFootprint introduces storage footprint metrics for LLM agent evaluation, revealing hidden disk usage, relevant for comprehensive medical agent benchmarking.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 当前LLM智能体基准仅测量任务完成度和推理成本，忽略了运行后遗留的存储足迹（如日志、检查点）。本文提出AgentFootprint跨框架基准，设计序列化感知指标体系测量总保留量、重复量、增长量、压缩率等，并发现简单字节级测量会严重低估重复量。该方法为智能体评估提供了新的成本维度，在基于EHR的医疗智能体评估中，可用于衡量数据存储开销和合规审计负担，完善评估体系。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 当前LLM智能体基准仅测量任务完成度和推理成本，忽略了运行后遗留的存储足迹（如日志、检查点）。本文提出AgentFootprint跨框架基准，设计序列化感知指标体系测量总保留量、重复量、增长量、压缩率等，并发现简单字节级测量会严重低估重复量。该方法为智能体评估提供了新的成本维度，在基于EHR的医疗智能体评估中，可用于衡量数据存储开销和合规审计负担，完善评估体系。

quick_skim.title: MemoHarness: Agent Harnesses That Learn from Experience
quick_skim.abstract: An agent harness is the external control layer that turns a base LLM into an executable agent by managing context, tools, orchestration, memory, decoding, and output handling. While harness design strongly affects agent behavior, most automatic improvement methods optimize narrower artifacts such as prompts, pipelines, or workflows, and deployed agents usually reuse a single global harness for all cases. We introduce MemoHarness, an adaptive harness optimization framework that learns from its own executions. MemoHarness decomposes the harness into six editable control dimensions, stores per-case diagnoses and distilled global patterns in a dual-layer experience bank, and adapts the learned harness to each test case using retrieved experience without test-time labels, feedback, or additional search. In our evaluation across shell-agent, code-generation, and analytical-reasoning benchmarks, MemoHarness improves over the fixed harnesses we compare against and shows selective transfer to unseen suites and base models. Its additional context can also remain cost-competitive when much of the retrieved experience is cacheable. These results provide evidence that execution experience is a practical substrate for building agent harnesses that are more adaptive than a single static configuration, while leaving broader claims about statistical robustness and component attribution to future work.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: agent harness optimization framework transferable to medical agent benchmarking
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 可迁移至医疗智能体评估的智能体框架优化方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 可迁移至医疗智能体评估的智能体框架优化方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 可迁移至医疗智能体评估的智能体框架优化方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MemoHarness proposes an adaptive harness optimization for LLM agents, learning from experience to improve agent behavior, applicable to medical agent evaluation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有的智能体框架通常为所有场景使用单一全局配置，缺乏自适应优化能力。本文提出MemoHarness，一种可学习的智能体框架优化框架：它将框架分解为六个可编辑的控制维度，通过双层经验库存储案例诊断和全局模式，并在测试时无需标签地检索经验来适应每个测试案例。实验表明该方法能有效提升智能体任务性能。该技术可迁移至基于EHR的医疗智能体评估，帮助自动优化医疗智能体的行为。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有的智能体框架通常为所有场景使用单一全局配置，缺乏自适应优化能力。本文提出MemoHarness，一种可学习的智能体框架优化框架：它将框架分解为六个可编辑的控制维度，通过双层经验库存储案例诊断和全局模式，并在测试时无需标签地检索经验来适应每个测试案例。实验表明该方法能有效提升智能体任务性能。该技术可迁移至基于EHR的医疗智能体评估，帮助自动优化医疗智能体的行为。

quick_skim.title: MCPEvol-Bench: Benchmarking LLM Agent Performance Across Dynamic Evolutions of MCP Servers
quick_skim.abstract: As Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers emerge as the core infrastructure for connecting LLMs with external tools, existing benchmarks leverage real-world MCP servers to evaluate LLM agents' tool-using capabilities. However, these benchmarks overlook the continuous evolution of tool interfaces and functionalities within MCP servers, resulting in flawed assessments that fail to capture the agent's adaptability in changing tool landscapes. To bridge this gap, we introduce \textbf{MCPEvol-Bench}, a novel benchmark for evaluating the task-solving capabilities of LLM agents under dynamic toolset evolution. Inspired by large-scale empirical study, we propose 11 mutation operators to simulate realistic tool evolution within 123 MCP servers. We benchmark 12 state-of-the-art LLMs on multiple versions of MCP servers, revealing that even frontier models struggle to adapt to evolving tools. For instance, GPT-5.4 and Claude-Sonnet-4-6 exhibit performance declines of 13.7\% and 14.4\% in evolved MCP servers, respectively, accompanied by substantial increases in planning and reasoning errors. These findings highlight the vulnerability of LLM-driven workflows, establishing MCPEvol-Bench as a standard for evaluating agent adaptability in dynamic tool environments.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: benchmark for LLM agents under dynamic tool evolution, applicable to medical agent benchmarking
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向动态工具演化的LLM智能体基准，可应用于医疗智能体评估
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向动态工具演化的LLM智能体基准，可应用于医疗智能体评估
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向动态工具演化的LLM智能体基准，可应用于医疗智能体评估
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MCPEvol-Bench introduces a benchmark evaluating LLM agents under evolving tool interfaces, providing a framework adaptable to medical agent evaluation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有LLM智能体基准忽略了工具接口和功能的连续演化，导致评估无法反映智能体在变化环境中的适应能力。本文提出MCPEvol-Bench，基于大规模实证研究设计11种变异算子模拟真实工具演化，在123个MCP服务器上构建动态基准。实验表明当前最先进的智能体在动态工具集上性能显著下降。该基准方法可直接迁移至基于EHR的医疗智能体评估，例如评估智能体在EHR系统接口更新时的适应能力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有LLM智能体基准忽略了工具接口和功能的连续演化，导致评估无法反映智能体在变化环境中的适应能力。本文提出MCPEvol-Bench，基于大规模实证研究设计11种变异算子模拟真实工具演化，在123个MCP服务器上构建动态基准。实验表明当前最先进的智能体在动态工具集上性能显著下降。该基准方法可直接迁移至基于EHR的医疗智能体评估，例如评估智能体在EHR系统接口更新时的适应能力。
